title: Hardening pass: task-store findings from TASK-001 + TASK-002 reviews
description:
Follow-up polish for the local task store adapter (`src/task-store.js`) and the new `atomicWriteFiles` two-phase primitive. None of these block correctness today, but each closes a real edge case.

1. **Partial-commit window in `atomicWriteFiles` (src/atomic-write.js:116-139)** — if the second rename in a batch fails after the first rename succeeded, on-disk state is split (task file new, index file old). Acceptable today because `index.json` is regenerable, but a recovery path should detect task/index drift on read. Suggested fix: a `verifyAndRepairIndex(repoRoot)` helper invoked at the top of `listTodos`, or a standalone sweeper.
2. **TOCTOU between `readAllTasks` and `atomicWriteFiles` (src/task-store.js:100-111)** — if a sibling task file is mutated between the read and the index regen, the regenerated `index.json` will project the older sibling. No concurrency safety is in scope for single-orchestrator use, but a one-line code comment naming the assumption belongs in the file.
3. **No recovery sweeper for `tasks/*.tmp.*`** — `recovery.sweepAndRecover` is bundle-scoped. A crash mid-write could leave orphan tmp files in `tasks/`. Extend the sweeper (or add a `tasks/`-scoped analog) and call it at orchestrator start.
4. **`listTodos` ignores `depends_on`** — AC1 of TASK-001 is satisfied by status filtering alone, but the orchestrator will eventually need a `listReady()` (or filter param) that excludes tasks whose dependencies are not yet `done`.
5. **No defensive ajv validation on read** — `readAllTasks` does `JSON.parse` only. A malformed task file would surface as a downstream field error rather than a schema-validated rejection. Add validation-on-write (cheaper than validation-on-read) using the existing schema and the ajv pattern from `tests/live-state.spec.js`.
6. **Lexical sort mis-orders TASK-1000 against TASK-999 in `buildIndexBytes` (src/task-store.js:72) and `listTodos` (src/task-store.js:85)** — the comparator `a.key < b.key` is a string compare, so once a 4-digit key exists, `index.json` will list `TASK-1000` before `TASK-999` (because `'1' < '9'`). MEDIUM finding from the TASK-002 reviewer. Pre-existing from TASK-001's code, made visible by the TASK-002 padding-boundary test which only checks the new key string and not its index position. Fix: numeric compare on the trailing digits, e.g. `(a, b) => parseInt(a.key.slice(5), 10) - parseInt(b.key.slice(5), 10)`. Add a regression test that puts `TASK-999` + `TASK-1000` in the store and asserts the index lists them in `999, 1000` order.

References: full reviewer reports are summarized in the TASK-001 closing comment (2026-05-25T06:30:00Z) and the TASK-002 closing comment (2026-05-25T07:30:00Z).
acceptance_criteria: `atomicWriteFiles` either becomes truly all-or-nothing or is paired with an automatic drift-detect-and-repair path that fires before `listTodos` returns stale data.
`src/task-store.js` carries an explicit single-writer assumption comment near the read-then-write block, and any future multi-writer requirement is captured as a follow-up ticket.
An orchestrator-start hook (or equivalent) removes orphan `tasks/*.tmp.*` files left behind by interrupted writes.
`listTodos` (or a new `listReady`) excludes tasks whose `depends_on` keys are not yet `done`; existing `listTodos` behavior may stay for raw status listing if both are kept.

Writes via `transitionStatus`, `appendComment`, and `createTask` validate the resulting payload against `tasks/schema.json` before committing; invalid payloads throw and leave on-disk bytes unchanged.

Both `buildIndexBytes` and `listTodos` sort by the trailing numeric portion of the key (not the full string), so `TASK-999` appears before `TASK-1000` in `index.json` and `listTodos` output; a regression test enforces this.

`createTask` self-bootstraps `tasks/` via `mkdirSync(tasksDir, {recursive: true})` before the first atomic write so callers on a fresh repo (no prior task store) don't ENOENT. Drop the workaround `mkdirSync` in `src/backlog-seeder.js` once this AC lands.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the TASK-001 reviewer audit (verdict APPROVE, 5 LOWs). All five concern the task-store adapter or its underlying atomic-write batched primitive. Take alongside or after TASK-008 (which carries 5 LOWs from the TASK-004 review).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Added AC6 from the TASK-002 reviewer audit: lexical-sort bug in `buildIndexBytes`/`listTodos` that mis-orders `TASK-1000` against `TASK-999`. Classified MEDIUM by the reviewer but pre-existing from TASK-001 (TASK-002 didn't introduce it, only made it visible). Same module, same fix profile — rolled in rather than spawning a separate ticket. AC5 was also broadened to cover `createTask` since it's a third write path that should validate before committing.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Added AC7 from the TASK-014 reviewer audit: createTask must self-bootstrap tasks/ via mkdirSync so first-time callers on a fresh repo (notably backlog-seeder, but also bin/new-task.js on a wiped repo) do not ENOENT. The TASK-014 impl dev paper-mached the issue by adding the mkdir in src/backlog-seeder.js itself; that workaround is to be removed once AC7 lands. Reviewer classified MEDIUM. Same module + same fix profile as the other 6 ACs already here.
